You are a blind ceiling-quality judge. You receive one task and three anonymized candidate patches. Do not infer or mention model, arm, attempt, harness, or author identity; judge only the task text and diffs.

Task text:
Add support for item_comments to syndication framework
Description
	
Add comments argument to feed.add_item() in syndication.views so that item_comments can be defined directly without having to take the detour via item_extra_kwargs .
Additionally, comments is already explicitly mentioned in the feedparser, but not implemented in the view.

P1 patch.diff:
```diff
diff --git a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
index 68ffb0bfe0..a531fcfc3a 100644
--- a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
+++ b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
@@ -199,10 +199,17 @@ class Feed:
             if updateddate and is_naive(updateddate):
                 updateddate = make_aware(updateddate, tz)
 
+            item_extra_kwargs = dict(self.item_extra_kwargs(item))
+            if 'comments' in item_extra_kwargs:
+                comments = item_extra_kwargs.pop('comments')
+            else:
+                comments = self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item)
+
             feed.add_item(
                 title=title,
                 link=link,
                 description=description,
+                comments=comments,
                 unique_id=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_guid', item, link),
                 unique_id_is_permalink=self._get_dynamic_attr(
                     'item_guid_is_permalink', item),
@@ -214,6 +221,6 @@ class Feed:
                 author_link=author_link,
                 categories=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_categories', item),
                 item_copyright=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_copyright', item),
-                **self.item_extra_kwargs(item)
+                **item_extra_kwargs
             )
         return feed
diff --git a/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt b/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
index 13aa944e8a..993f1e2ff8 100644
--- a/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
+++ b/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
@@ -673,6 +673,22 @@ This example illustrates all possible attributes and methods for a
             Returns the URL for every item in the feed.
             """
 
+        # ITEM COMMENTS URL -- One of the following three is optional. This is
+        # only applicable to RSS feeds.
+
+        def item_comments(self, item):
+            """
+            Takes an item, as returned by items(), and returns the item's
+            comments URL as a normal Python string.
+            """
+
+        def item_comments(self):
+            """
+            Returns the comments URL for every item in the feed.
+            """
+
+        item_comments = 'https://www.example.com/comments/' # Hard-coded comments URL.
+
         # ITEM_GUID -- The following method is optional. If not provided, the
         # item's link is used by default.

--- a/tests/syndication_tests/feeds.py
+++ b/tests/syndication_tests/feeds.py
@@ -49,6 +49,18 @@ class TestRss2FeedWithGuidIsPermaLinkFalse(TestRss2Feed):
         return False
 
 
+class TestRss2FeedWithComments(TestRss2Feed):
+    def item_comments(self, item):
+        return 'http://example.com/blog/%s/comments/' % item.pk
+
+
+class TestRss2FeedWithCommentsFromExtraKwargs(TestRss2Feed):
+    def item_extra_kwargs(self, item):
+        return {
+            'comments': 'http://example.com/blog/%s/comments/' % item.pk,
+        }
+
+
 class TestRss091Feed(TestRss2Feed):
     feed_type = feedgenerator.RssUserland091Feed

+    def test_rss2_feed_with_comments(self):
+        response = self.client.get('/syndication/rss2/comments/')
+        doc = minidom.parseString(response.content)
+        items = doc.getElementsByTagName('channel')[0].getElementsByTagName('item')
+
+        self.assertChildNodeContent(items[0], {
+            'comments': 'http://example.com/blog/1/comments/',
+        })
+
+    def test_rss2_feed_with_comments_from_extra_kwargs(self):
+        response = self.client.get('/syndication/rss2/comments-extra/')
+        doc = minidom.parseString(response.content)
+        items = doc.getElementsByTagName('channel')[0].getElementsByTagName('item')
+
+        self.assertChildNodeContent(items[0], {
+            'comments': 'http://example.com/blog/1/comments/',
+        })
+
     def test_rss2_single_enclosure(self):
         response = self.client.get('/syndication/rss2/single-enclosure/')
         doc = minidom.parseString(response.content)
```

strict_win_deltas.delta: P1 adds an explicit `comments=comments` keyword to `feed.add_item()`, while P2 only injects `'comments'` into `item_extra_kwargs` before expanding it.
P1 removes `'comments'` from `item_extra_kwargs` before expansion, whereas P3 adds `comments=...` while still expanding `**self.item_extra_kwargs(item)`.
P2 checks whether `'comments'` is already in `item_extra_kwargs` before adding the item_comments-derived value, while P3 unconditionally passes both `comments=...` and all extra kwargs.
P1 adds a dedicated `TestRss2FeedWithCommentsFromExtraKwargs` route and test for the existing `item_extra_kwargs({'comments': ...})` path, while P2 only tests the new `item_comments()` hook.
P1 preserves the old extra-kwargs comments path by popping it before `**item_extra_kwargs`, while P3 would pass duplicate `comments` keywords for that same feed shape.
P2 avoids duplicate comments keywords by not adding one when `'comments'` is already present in `item_extra_kwargs`, but P3 has no such guard.
P1 implements the requested first-class `comments` argument in the `feed.add_item()` call and keeps the existing detour working, while P2 keeps comments hidden inside `**item_extra_kwargs`.

P1 documents the hook as a comments URL applicable to RSS feeds and tests both direct and extra-kwargs paths, while P3's test value is a copyright string and its code conflicts with the existing extra-kwargs path.
P2's new hook returns a comments URL and preserves existing extra-kwargs comments when present, while P3 uses `'Copyright (c) 2007, Sally Smith'` as `item_comments` in the shared RSS2 test feed.
P1 keeps `comments` as a named local and named `feed.add_item()` keyword, making precedence explicit, whereas P2 represents the first-class API field through the generic kwargs bag.
P1 confines comments coverage to dedicated feed classes and routes, while P3 changes the base `TestRss2Feed` so every RSS2 test item gains a comments child.
P2 keeps the base test feed unchanged and adds a narrow comments feed, whereas P3 folds comments into the shared `TestRss2Feed` fixture with a non-URL value.

Task text:
Add support for item_comments to syndication framework
Description
	
Add comments argument to feed.add_item() in syndication.views so that item_comments can be defined directly without having to take the detour via item_extra_kwargs .
Additionally, comments is already explicitly mentioned in the feedparser, but not implemented in the view.

P1 patch.diff:
```diff
diff --git a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py

@@ -199,10 +199,17 @@ class Feed:

                 updateddate = make_aware(updateddate, tz)
 
+            item_extra_kwargs = dict(self.item_extra_kwargs(item))
+            if 'comments' in item_extra_kwargs:
+                comments = item_extra_kwargs.pop('comments')
+            else:
+                comments = self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item)
+
             feed.add_item(
                 title=title,
                 link=link,
                 description=description,
+                comments=comments,
                 unique_id=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_guid', item, link),
                 unique_id_is_permalink=self._get_dynamic_attr(
```
